operator_explanation.current_status: Blocked evidence record for distributed successor/comparator wording.
operator_explanation.plain_language: TideFS has no current evidence-backed permission to tell an operator that its distributed behavior is an OpenZFS, Ceph, DRBD, clustered-storage, successor, superior, or comparator-performance equivalent.
what_is_proven: The registered distributed successor evidence paths were reviewed for this claim.
The current artifact set records the missing distributed proof and keeps the claim blocked.
what_is_not_proven: Distributed data availability across node, process, network, or device faults.
Membership, fencing, split-brain exclusion, or ownership transfer for product data.
TCP, RDMA, loopback, or mixed transport performance and fault behavior.
Recovery, rebuild, scrub, re-replication, or degraded-visible client behavior.
Any OpenZFS, Ceph, DRBD, clustered-storage, successor, superiority, or comparator-performance comparison.
operator_explanation.operator_visible_boundary: Operator-facing text must continue to present distributed successor/comparator behavior as unproven and blocked until a future claim validation supplies current multi-process distributed evidence for the exact envelope.
non_comparable_cases: Any current benchmark, prototype, or source-model row that does not name topology, membership, transport, media, workload, attribution, fault model, and degraded/recovery state.
Any comparison against OpenZFS, Ceph, DRBD, or clustered-storage behavior without an evidence-backed incumbent setup and matching TideFS envelope.
Any release-readiness or product-admission inference from these files alone.
